channel.title: Daily GovCon Briefing
channel.description: Daily synthesis of federal contracting news — acquisition policy, DoD and VA, federal AI policy — and what it means for contractors. Five to seven minutes. One voice. Weekdays.
channel.itunes:author: Mike Ross
channel.itunes:summary: Daily synthesis of federal contracting news and what it means for contractors.

item.title: GovCon today: June 12, 2026 — NITAAC's Final Gate Closes
item.description: NITAAC has confirmed the sunset of its entire GWAC portfolio — CIO-SP3, CIO-SP3 Small Business, and CIO-CS — with October 29, 2026 as the hard stop for new order awards. For small AI contractors, this is a structural, irreversible narrowing of the task order market, not a political headwind that can be unwound. The firms that clear the next decade of federal AI work are the ones already on Polaris or holding Alliant 2 teaming arrangements — vehicle position is now the primary competitive differentiator, ahead of technical capability.
item.content:encoded:
NITAAC's Final Gate Closes

NITAAC has confirmed the sunset of its entire GWAC portfolio — CIO-SP3, CIO-SP3 Small Business, and CIO-CS — with October 29, 2026 as the hard stop for new order awards. For small AI contractors, this is a structural, irreversible narrowing of the task order market, not a political headwind that can be unwound. The firms that clear the next decade of federal AI work are the ones already on Polaris or holding Alliant 2 teaming arrangements — vehicle position is now the primary competitive differentiator, ahead of technical capability.

Featured story

  • NIH contracting arm announces sunset of all governmentwide vehicles — FedScoop

Also today

  • White House discussions are weighing giving CISA Mythos access — Washington Technology
  • AI can save veterans' time, but TMF head says reauthorization needed for more investments — FedScoop
  • Senate pushes DOD to create new combatant command for unmanned systems — DefenseScoop
  • SASC's $1.14T defense policy bill creates combatant command for drones — Breaking Defense
  • White House polices hitting small business contractors hard, Senator says — Federal News Network
  • Air Force Releases Draft RFP for TETRAS III R&D Support IDIQ — GovConWire
  • Navy Taps 59 Companies for Potential $250M Logistics IT Modernization Contract — GovConWire
  • Army commissions 3 more tech executives into Detachment 201 — DefenseScoop

item.title: GovCon today: June 11, 2026 — The Mythos Governance Freeze
item.description: The White House hasn't told agencies how to access or deploy Anthropic's Mythos model — and that silence is now colliding with CISA's hard-deadline vulnerability-patching mandates, creating a mandate-without-tool condition that is a direct pricing and scheduling risk for any AI shop bidding on federal cyber work. Meanwhile, GWAC consolidation is accelerating and SBA on-ramps are disappearing: the channel problem is real, but total federal IT spend isn't shrinking — it's being redistributed toward large platforms and surviving vehicles.
item.content:encoded:
The Mythos Governance Freeze

The White House hasn't told agencies how to access or deploy Anthropic's Mythos model — and that silence is now colliding with CISA's hard-deadline vulnerability-patching mandates, creating a mandate-without-tool condition that is a direct pricing and scheduling risk for any AI shop bidding on federal cyber work. Meanwhile, GWAC consolidation is accelerating and SBA on-ramps are disappearing: the channel problem is real, but total federal IT spend isn't shrinking — it's being redistributed toward large platforms and surviving vehicles.

Featured story

  • Lack of White House guidance has complicated agency Mythos adoption, people familiar say — Washington Technology

Also today

  • CISA directive revamps how agencies prioritize vulnerable systems — Nextgov/FCW `[1a605b6c2b]`
  • CISA directive orders agencies to prioritize vulnerability patching in a new way — FedScoop `[922d754cf0]`
  • AI directive focuses patching efforts on 'highest risk' vulnerabilities — Federal News Network `[52ed252618]`
  • The AI arms race everyone's ignoring — Federal News Network `[a7e20d7fdf]`
  • Pentagon announces 'Cyber Mastery Incentive Pay' — DefenseScoop `[e73abd655a]`
  • Ensuring seamless access in contested environments with zero trust — Federal News Network `[33c634edbc]`
  • NIH pulls the plug on its government-wide contracts — Washington Technology `[9703b81406]`
  • GSA Names 17 Additional Apparently Successful Offerors for Polaris SDVOSB Pool — GovConWire `[cdfaa958f3]`
  • GSA gets moving on second batch of Polaris SDVOSB awards — Washington Technology `[42b6f430cb]`
  • Innovation on ice: How DOGE derailed an SBA tech program — FedScoop `[413765e377]`
  • OPM's long-planned HR overhaul moves ahead with $396M award to Oracle — Government Executive `[591bcd4898]`
  • House appropriators release $1 trillion defense bill for FY27 — Breaking Defense `[fe3546b315]`
  • US seizes alleged China-linked sites targeting security clearance holders — Nextgov/FCW `[9165de7039]`
